title: quercus libani
intro: Quercus libani, also known as the Lebanese oak or Mount Lebanon oak, is a deciduous tree species native to the mountainous regions of Lebanon and Syria. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Arabic medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions. The leaves and bark of Quercus libani contain flavonoids, tannins, and other bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help in reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Quercus libani include teas, infusions, and decoctions that can be used to address a range of health issues, from digestive problems to respiratory infections.
